$schema_note: Role definitions and full permission matrix. Legend: C=Create, R=Read, U=Update, D=Delete(soft), X=Execute/Special, own=own records only, dept=department scope

roles.name: Super Admin
roles.description: System owner with unrestricted access, including settings, users, terms, module flags, and audit logs.
hard_rules: Only role that edits system settings and module flags

roles.name: Department Admin
roles.description: Manages department data and operations: students, faculty, subjects, sections, offerings, schedules, enrollments, correction approvals, and department reports.
hard_rules: Scope limited to the IT department

roles.name: Faculty
roles.description: Operates class sessions and attendance for assigned offerings only.
hard_rules: Actions restricted to offerings assigned to them in the active term
Can mark manual attendance for students with technical issues

roles.name: Student
roles.account_source: OSAS API (SSO with university credentials; no local registration)
roles.description: Scans attendance, views own records, files correction requests.
hard_rules: Login requires active/current OSAS status — alumni and inactive students rejected
Can only ever read own data
Cannot modify any attendance record directly

roles.name: Guest
roles.description: Public landing page and login only.

attendance_records.FACULTY: RU (own classes, reason required) + C (manual entry)

enforcement.backend: authenticate → authorize(roles) → ownership guard (service layer verifies faculty↔offering and student↔self) on every protected route; backend is the single source of truth
enforcement.frontend: Route guards + conditional UI mirror the matrix for UX only
enforcement.escalation: No role can grant itself permissions; role changes are Super Admin actions and audited
